workflow.name: Triage and escalate tenant complaints from Gmail or forms to Slack with Claude AI
workflow.description:
### How it works
1. Complaints arrive via Gmail or a web form webhook
2. Claude AI classifies each complaint: fault category, priority (P1/P2/P3), tenant tone, and drafts an acknowledgement email
3. The right technician is looked up in Airtable by fault category
4. A work order is created and the tenant receives an ACK email with their ticket reference and SLA commitment
5. The FM team is notified in Slack with ticket summary
6. An hourly schedule checks open tickets — any past their SLA deadline trigger an urgent escalation to FM management


### How to set up
1. Connect Gmail to the **Gmail Trigger** and **Send ACK email** nodes
2. Create your Airtable base with a **Complaints** table and a **Technician** table (one row per fault category)
3. Connect Airtable, Anthropic, and Slack in their respective nodes
4. If using a web form, point it to the Webhook URL

values.content:
You are an FM operations assistant for a Singapore commercial building. Extract structured data from tenant complaint text. Tenant base is multilingual (English, Mandarin, Singlish) — always output English.

Return ONLY valid JSON with these exact fields:
{
  "unit": "unit number e.g. #05-12, or 'unknown' if not mentioned",
  "fault_category": "one of: ACMV | Plumbing | Electrical | Cleanliness | Access | Noise",
  "urgency_indicators": "brief string of urgency signals found in text",
  "tenant_tone": "one of: Frustrated | Neutral | Urgent | Abusive",
  "recurrence_signal": Yes or No,
  "priority_tier": "one of: P1 | P2 | P3",
  "summary": "one sentence plain English summary",
  "ack_email_body": "professional acknowledgement, salutation then empty line then body, max 120 words, tone-matched to tenant, no sign-off"
}

Priority rules:
- P1 (2hr SLA): safety hazard, flooding, fire risk, lift entrapment, total power failure multi-unit
- P2 (4hr SLA): single-unit failure significantly impacting operations
- P3 (24hr SLA): minor inconvenience, cosmetic, low urgency

Fault category rules:
- ACMV: aircon, ventilation, airflow, cooling, fan coil, freezing, not cold
- Plumbing: water leak, tap, toilet, drainage, flooding from pipes
- Electrical: power trip, lights, sockets, circuit breaker, no power
- Cleanliness: rubbish, pest, cockroach, smell, dirty, bin overflow
- Access: door, lift, gate, key card, security, intercom, barrier
- Noise: noise, banging, drilling, music, vibration, loud neighbour

parameters.message:
={{ $('Parse LLM output').item.json.ack_email_body + '

Ticket reference: ' + $('Parse LLM output').item.json.ticket_id + '
Expected response: within ' + $('Parse LLM output').item.json.sla_hours + ' hour(s) — by ' + new Date($('Parse LLM output').item.json.sla_deadline).toLocaleString('en-SG', { timeZone: 'Asia/Singapore', dateStyle: 'short', timeStyle: 'short' }) + ' SGT.

FM Management Team' }}
parameters.subject: ={{ '[' + $('Parse LLM output').item.json.ticket_id + '] Complaint received — ' + $('Parse LLM output').item.json.fault_category }}

parameters.content:
## FM Complaint Triage & SLA Escalation                                                                                                                                                                             
Facility management teams waste hours manually reading tenant emails, deciding urgency, and chasing overdue tickets. This workflow automates the entire intake-to-dispatch loop from complaint received to technician notified.

               
### Who's it for                                                                                                                                            
FM managers and operations teams in commercial buildings who receive tenant complaints by email or web form.


### How it works
1. Complaints arrive via Gmail or a web form webhook
2. Claude AI classifies each complaint: fault category, priority (P1/P2/P3), tenant tone, and drafts an acknowledgement email
3. The right technician is looked up in Airtable by fault category
4. A work order is created and the tenant receives an ACK email with their ticket reference and SLA commitment
5. The FM team is notified in Slack with ticket summary
6. An hourly schedule checks open tickets — any past their SLA deadline trigger an urgent escalation to FM management


### How to set up
1. Connect Gmail to the **Gmail Trigger** and **Send ACK email** nodes
2. Create your Airtable base with a **Complaints** table and a **Technician** table (one row per fault category)
3. Connect Airtable, Anthropic, and Slack in their respective nodes
4. If using a web form, point it to the Webhook URL


### Requirements
- Gmail account
- Airtable account
- Anthropic API key
- Slack workspace.


### How to customize the workflow
- Edit the LLM system prompt and the **Parse LLM output** node to change fault categories or SLA hours.
- Point the escalation node to a separate management-only Slack channel if needed. 
- Remove the Webhook trigger and **Normalise form inputs** node if you only use email.

**1. Complaint ingestion**

Accepts complaints from two sources:
- **Gmail Trigger** polls your shared FM inbox every minute for new INBOX messages
- **Webhook** receives POST requests from a web form (Tally, Typeform, Power Automate, etc.)

Both paths are normalised into identical fields (source, raw_complaint, tenant_email, tenant_name, received_at) and merged into a single item before classification.

**2. AI triage (Claude Sonnet)**

Claude classifies the complaint and returns structured JSON:
- Fault category: ACMV | Plumbing | Electrical | Cleanliness | Access | Noise
- Priority: P1 (2hr SLA) | P2 (4hr SLA) | P3 (24hr SLA)
- Tenant tone: Frustrated | Neutral | Urgent | Abusive
- Recurrence signal, urgency indicators, plain-English summary
- Draft acknowledgement email (tone-matched, max 120 words)

The Parse LLM output node strips markdown fences, parses JSON, generates a ticket ID (FM-XXXXXX), and calculates the SLA deadline timestamp.

**3. Technician assignment & work order**

Looks up the on-call technician for the fault category from your Airtable Technician table, then creates a full work order in the Complaints table.

The Technician table must have at least one row per fault category (ACMV, Plumbing, Electrical, Cleanliness, Access, Noise) with Technician Name and Technician Email columns.

**4. Tenant acknowledgement and dispatch**

Sends the AI-drafted acknowledgement email to the tenant with:
- Ticket reference (FM-XXXXXX)
- SLA commitment (e.g. "within 4 hours — by 3:45 PM SGT")

Acknowledgement Sent is then updated to true in Airtable so the SLA monitor knows not to double-escalate on a fresh ticket.

On-call technician is dispatched via an AI-drafted Slack message.

**5. Hourly SLA monitoring**

Runs independently on a 1-hour schedule. Fetches all open tickets from Airtable, loops through each one, and checks whether the SLA Deadline has passed.

Any breached ticket triggers an urgent Slack message to the FM management channel with ticket details, assigned technician, and current status.

Tickets are only removed from the escalation loop once their Status is set to "Closed" in Airtable.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
